misra_context.description: Non-Boolean if-controlling expression
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 14.4 (Required): Non-Boolean if-controlling expression. The controlling expression of the 'if' statement is 'count', an integer, not an essentially Boolean type. Using non-Boolean values as conditions reduces readability and may mask logic errors where the value is non-zero but not the intended condition.

misra_context.description: Controlling expressions shall not be invariant
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 14.3 (Required): Controlling expressions shall not be invariant. This code violates Rule 14.3 because controlling expressions shall not be invariant. Loop and condition forms should make control flow explicit, safe, and reviewable.

misra_context.description: A switch expression shall not have essentially Boolean type
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 16.7 (Required): A switch expression shall not have essentially Boolean type. This code violates Rule 16.7 because a switch expression shall not have essentially Boolean type. Switch logic must be complete, explicit, and easy to follow.

This code violates Rule 20.9 because all identifiers used in the controlling expression of #if or #elif shall be #define'd before evaluation. Preprocessor misuse can obscure intent and create fragile expansions.

misra_context.description: The relational operators > >= < <= shall not be applied to objects of pointer type except where they point into the same array
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 18.3 (Required): The relational operators > >= < <= shall not be applied to objects of pointer type except where they point into the same array. misra_context.description: A function parameter should not be modified
All declarations of an object or function shall use the same names and type qualifiers
misra_context.guidelines:

misra_context.description: Implicit narrowing conversion
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 10.3 (Required): Implicit narrowing conversion. The value of 'u16val' (uint16_t) is assigned to 'u8val' (uint8_t). This implicit narrowing conversion may silently truncate the value (300 → 44), causing data loss and incorrect program behaviour.

misra_context.description: The value of an expression should not be cast to an inappropriate essential type
misra_context.guidelines:
uint32_t wide = 300U;
uint8_t  small = (uint8_t)wide;   /* cast to inappropriate essential type */

misra_context.description: The lowercase character "l" shall not be used in a literal suffix
misra_context.guidelines: long value = 10l;   /* lowercase l suffix */

misra_context.description: The value of a composite expression shall not be cast to a different essential type category or a wider essential type
misra_context.guidelines:
uint16_t a, b;
uint32_t x = (uint32_t)(a + b);   /* cast composite expression to wider category */

misra_context.description: Non-initialized local variable
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 9.1 (Mandatory): Non-initialized local variable. Variable 'x' is read before being assigned a value. Reading an automatic variable before it is set leads to undefined behaviour and unpredictable results.
